data.narrative:
Telexistence, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jin Tomioka, Ken Tomoyose, and Susumu Tachi, has raised $170M+ to date. The peer universe comprises nine comparable companies: one public entity (277810.KQ) and eight private firms including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity, all at Series B stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base of $170M and applying a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $323M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the robotics sector at this stage; notably, 1X Technologies commands a $10B valuation while Fairmat stands at $350M.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $275M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $699M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects material uncertainty around Telexistence's competitive positioning and revenue traction relative to the peer set. Key sensitivities include (1) actual revenue multiples versus peer benchmarks, (2) market differentiation in hardware robotics, and (3) path to Series C funding. The peer range itself—spanning one order of magnitude—underscores sector-wide valuation volatility at this stage.
